articles.title: E8 Properties Converts Retail Spaces Into Family Entertainment Centers
articles.summary: Real estate firm E8 Properties is actively repurposing vacant or underperforming retail locations into family entertainment centers, reflecting the broader trend of experiential tenants filling the void left by traditional retail. This strategy signals continued investor and developer confidence in the FEC model as an anchor use for commercial real estate. FEC operators and developers should take note of the deal structures and conversion playbook being deployed.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Strong Investment Trends
articles.summary: A new market report highlights growing investment activity and emerging trends in the location-based entertainment sector, which encompasses FECs, VR attractions, and immersive experiences. The data points to sustained post-pandemic demand and increasing institutional interest in the space. FEC investors and operators can use these insights to benchmark growth strategies and identify expansion opportunities.

articles.title: FEC of the Year Hosts AAMA's FEC Connect Industry Event
articles.summary: The AAMA's FEC Connect gathering was hosted by the FEC of the Year winner, bringing together operators and suppliers for industry networking and education. Events like FEC Connect are key touchpoints for staying current on best practices, new products, and peer benchmarking. FEC operators should track AAMA programming for upcoming learning and networking opportunities.

articles.title: $3 Billion Motown Sports Village Planned Near Detroit Metro Airport
articles.summary: An ambitious $3 billion mixed-use entertainment destination is planned near Detroit Metro Airport, incorporating an arena, water park, hotels, IMAX theater, and more. Projects of this scale signal the continued appetite for large-format destination entertainment complexes that blend multiple FEC-adjacent attractions. Suppliers and operators in the region should monitor this development for potential partnership and anchor tenant opportunities.

articles.title: Cinemark's Gamescape FEC Concept Canceled at Merriam Town Center
articles.summary: Cinemark's planned Gamescape entertainment center at Merriam Town Center in Kansas has been scrapped, representing a notable setback for the theater chain's FEC diversification strategy. The cancellation raises questions about site selection challenges and the financial viability of cinema-adjacent FEC concepts in certain markets. Operators and developers evaluating similar hybrid entertainment projects should examine the factors behind this decision.

articles.title: Mattel-Branded Indoor Waterpark Planned for Weldon Mills Resort
articles.summary: A Mattel Wonder-branded indoor waterpark is set to be incorporated into the planned Weldon Mills Resort, extending the reach of IP-licensed family entertainment into resort hospitality. The move reflects growing momentum for branded indoor waterpark attractions tied to recognizable children's properties. FEC operators should watch this trend as IP-driven indoor water attractions increasingly compete for family leisure spending.

articles.title: Supercharged Eyeing Polaris Entertainment District Alongside Topgolf and Smash Park
articles.summary: Supercharged, an entertainment venue concept, is considering entering the Polaris district in Ohio, which already hosts Topgolf and Smash Park, further clustering experiential entertainment options in a single corridor. Entertainment districts like this are becoming powerful draws for consumers and demonstrate the value of co-locating FEC-style venues. Operators and real estate developers should consider how destination clustering affects traffic, competition, and site selection.

articles.title: Vintia Partners With Momants AI to Bring Conversational Ticketing to Attractions
articles.summary: Vintia has integrated Momants AI into its Enviso platform to enable guests to purchase, modify, and upgrade tickets through natural-language conversations across channels including WhatsApp, Instagram, and website chatbots. This development lowers friction in the booking process and could meaningfully increase conversion rates and upsell opportunities for FEC operators. Venues using Enviso or evaluating ticketing platforms should evaluate conversational AI capabilities as a differentiator.

articles.title: $60M Sports Complex Pitched as Economic Development Engine in Louisiana
articles.summary: Parish leaders in Louisiana are pushing a $60 million sports and entertainment complex as a driver of local economic development, reflecting the growing use of large-scale FEC and sports facility investments as anchors for community revitalization. Projects like this frequently incorporate FEC elements such as arcade zones, food and beverage, and party spaces alongside sports courts. Operators and suppliers active in the sports entertainment segment should monitor public-private development deals of this type.

articles.title: Slick City Indoor Slide Park Opening New Location in Milford This Summer
articles.summary: Slick City, the growing indoor slide park chain, is expanding with a new venue in Milford, continuing its rapid national rollout of a concept that directly competes in the FEC space. The brand's growth trajectory is worth watching for operators in trampoline and active-play segments who may face new competition. Suppliers to the indoor active entertainment market should also note Slick City as a prospective client.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center Opens in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new location in Carrollton, Georgia, adding another multi-attraction FEC to the Southeast market. New openings of this type provide useful benchmarking data on format, pricing, and attraction mix for operators in similar markets. Local and regional competitors should assess how Thunderzone's arrival affects the competitive landscape.

articles.title: New Tenpin Bowling and Entertainment Centre Opening in Blackpool This June
articles.summary: Tenpin is opening a new entertainment center in Blackpool, now accepting bookings ahead of its June launch, adding a multi-attraction bowling-anchored venue to a highly competitive UK leisure destination. The opening demonstrates continued operator confidence in combining bowling with broader entertainment offerings. FEC operators and suppliers in the UK bowling segment should track Tenpin's expansion pace and venue format.

articles.title: Indoor Play and Entertainment Booming as Families Seek Heat and Pollution Relief
articles.summary: Rising temperatures and urban air quality concerns are driving increased demand for indoor play and entertainment venues, according to industry observers, particularly in markets across South Asia. This macro trend supports the investment case for climate-controlled FEC formats including trampoline parks, arcades, and indoor active play. Operators with strong indoor offerings should lean into this messaging in their marketing strategies.

articles.title: $2M Permit Approved to Convert Former AMC Theater in Illinois Into New Entertainment Venue
articles.summary: A $2 million remodeling permit has been approved to transform a former AMC Theater in Marion, Illinois into a new entertainment facility, continuing the trend of repurposing large-format retail and cinema spaces for experiential use. The project is a case study in adaptive reuse that many FEC developers are exploring as theater footprints become available. Operators and investors evaluating similar conversion projects should monitor progress here for format insights.

articles.title: Former Grocery Store in Coldwater, Michigan to Become Barcade and Market
articles.summary: A shuttered Family Fare grocery store in Coldwater, Michigan is being converted into a barcade and market concept, illustrating the ongoing wave of adaptive reuse projects turning vacant retail into entertainment destinations. Barcade concepts that blend gaming, food, and beverage continue to gain traction as a viable FEC format for adult audiences. Operators eyeing underutilized retail real estate should follow this project as a model.

articles.title: Expert Guide: How to Design Truly Accessible Attractions From the Ground Up
articles.summary: A comprehensive expert guide urges attraction designers and operators to embed accessibility into the earliest concept stages rather than retrofitting later, emphasizing that 1.2 billion people globally have additional needs, making inclusion a major market opportunity. Key principles include designing for dignity with shared boarding, prioritizing predictability for neurodivergent guests, and involving disabled visitors in the design process from the start. FEC operators planning new builds or refurbishments should use these guidelines to future-proof their facilities and expand their addressable audience.

articles.title: London-Born Indoor Entertainment Concept With Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-origin indoor entertainment venue featuring virtual soccer, foosball, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, showcasing how tech-driven social entertainment concepts are expanding across European cities. The format blends physical and digital gameplay with food and beverage in a way highly relevant to FEC operators looking to differentiate their offer. Operators and suppliers should study this concept's attraction mix and hospitality integration as a design reference.

articles.title: Morey's Piers Kicks Off 2026 Season With New Attractions and Major Renovations
articles.summary: Morey's Piers in Wildwood, New Jersey has launched its 2026 season featuring new experiences, refreshed attractions, and significant infrastructure improvements including the refurbishment of its iconic Giant Wheel. The venue's seasonal refresh strategy offers useful benchmarking for FEC operators on how to generate renewed consumer interest and press coverage. Operators planning capital improvement cycles can look to Morey's staggered renovation and announcement approach as a model.

articles.title: UK Cuts VAT to 5% for Theme Parks and Attractions This Summer
articles.summary: The UK government has announced a temporary VAT reduction from 20% to 5% on eligible attraction tickets from June 25 to September 1 as part of a 'Great British Summer Savings' initiative designed to support families and attendance-dependent businesses. The cut could meaningfully lower effective ticket prices and stimulate visits to UK FECs, theme parks, and indoor entertainment venues during peak summer trading. UK-based operators should communicate this saving proactively in their marketing and pricing strategy.

articles.title: Industry Commentary: Theme Parks Should Be Transparent About Attendance Declines
articles.summary: A commentary piece argues that theme parks and attractions should be candid about the real reasons behind flagging attendance rather than deflecting or spinning the data, as transparency builds long-term consumer and investor trust. For FEC operators, the piece serves as a useful reminder that honest communication about performance challenges can be a strategic asset rather than a liability. The argument has direct relevance for operators navigating post-peak normalization in visitation.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Indoor Entertainment Location in Lucknow, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone, an indoor entertainment and play center brand, has expanded with a new flagship opening in Lucknow as part of its broader rollout across Indian tier-1 and tier-2 cities. The opening reflects accelerating growth in India's organized indoor entertainment sector, driven by urbanization and a large youth demographic. International FEC suppliers and operators looking at emerging markets should monitor India's indoor entertainment expansion closely.

articles.title: Wisconsin Dells Adds Medieval Resort and New Attractions for Summer 2026
articles.summary: Wisconsin Dells, a major Midwest entertainment destination, is adding a medieval-themed resort among several new attractions for the 2026 summer season, reinforcing the destination's strategy of continuous concept diversification. Theme-driven immersive resort concepts are increasingly relevant to FEC operators exploring resort-style expansion or branded environment design. Suppliers and designers in the themed entertainment space should note the continued investment appetite in this market.

articles.title: OKANA Resort Fully Open for Its First Summer Season in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: OKANA Resort in Oklahoma City is now fully operational heading into its inaugural summer season, offering a major new mixed-use entertainment and hospitality destination to the region. As a destination that likely incorporates water attractions, dining, and entertainment, OKANA's ramp-up performance will be watched closely by developers of similar resort-entertainment hybrids. FEC operators and investors interested in resort-scale entertainment destinations should track OKANA's operational debut.

articles.title: DreamLab Immersive Combines AI-Assisted Design With Themed Entertainment Production
articles.summary: DreamLab Immersive, an Orlando-based creative studio, is positioning itself as a leader in AI-assisted design and production for immersive environments including themed attractions, location-based experiences, and FEC-relevant interactive installations. The studio's multidisciplinary approach combining storytelling, digital media, and generative AI offers FEC operators a potential partner for next-generation attraction development. Operators planning immersive experience upgrades or new concept builds should evaluate AI-integrated design studios as part of their development team.

articles.title: America 250 Wheel to Make Midwest Debut at Wisconsin State Fair
articles.summary: The America 250 Wheel, the largest observation wheel ever to appear at Wisconsin State Fair Park, will be a marquee attraction at the fair's 175th anniversary celebration. Large-format observation wheels continue to prove their value as high-visibility signature attractions that drive ticket sales and media coverage. FEC operators and event venue managers should note the draw power of statement attractions like oversized wheels for seasonal events.

articles.title: Long Island Guide Highlights Arcades, Ax-Throwing, Play Centers as Indoor Fun Staples
articles.summary: A consumer guide to indoor entertainment on Long Island prominently features arcades, ax-throwing venues, and indoor play centers, reflecting how these FEC formats have become mainstream leisure options for families and young adults. Consumer guides of this nature drive organic discovery and visitation for venues that appear in them, making local media relationships valuable for FEC operators. The variety of formats highlighted also underscores the competitive density of the FEC market in suburban metro areas.

articles.title: Rising Temperatures Fueling Growth in Indoor Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: An industry expert cited in a Nigerian publication argues that climate-driven heat is becoming a structural driver of demand for indoor entertainment centers as consumers seek climate-controlled leisure options. While the commentary originates in the African market, the trend has global relevance for FEC operators who can position indoor venues as a refuge from extreme weather. Operators should consider incorporating weather-driven demand into their marketing and capacity planning strategies.

articles.title: Survey: 60% of Kids Want an Elaborate Birthday Party, Signaling Strong FEC Demand
articles.summary: Data from Chuck E. Cheese indicates that 60% of children desire an elaborate birthday party experience, reinforcing the importance of birthday packages as a core revenue driver for family entertainment centers. Birthday events are among the highest-margin revenue streams in the FEC business, making this consumer insight directly actionable for operators looking to grow group sales. FEC operators should ensure their birthday packages align with elevated consumer expectations for memorable, elaborate celebrations.

articles.title: SeaWorld Orlando Opens New Expedition Odyssey: Fire & Ice Arctic Attraction
articles.summary: SeaWorld Orlando has launched Expedition Odyssey: Fire & Ice, a new family attraction themed around an Arctic adventure, with an exclusive early opening for annual pass members before a Memorial Day general debut. The attraction's pass-member preview strategy is a proven technique for rewarding loyalty and generating buzz ahead of a public launch. FEC operators with loyalty or membership programs can draw lessons from SeaWorld's phased opening approach.
